title: Feature Request: Allow multiple Podman Machines to run
body:
### Feature request description

I would like to be able to run multiple `Podman` `Machines`.

```bash 
podman machine init vm1
podman machine init vm2
```

```bash
podman machine start vm1
Starting machine "vm1"
```

```bash 
podman machine start vm2
Starting machine "vm2"
Error: unable to start "vm2": vm1 already starting or running: only one VM can be active at a time
```

### Suggest potential solution

I can run two `Podman` `Virtual Machines` at the same time.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

N/A

### Additional context

I am developing against `/dev/net/tun` and need to be able to use it from multiple containers hence the need for multiple `Virtual Machines` as two processes cannot use the device at the same time.

comments.body: @baude I believe you are the one with the strong opinions about this limit.

Podman machine is only designed to run one machine at a time.  There are reasons deeper than podman, as some of the supporting applications do not do a great job of supporting this.

perhaps you have a compelling use case that is worth reconsideration.

@baude, I'd love to hear about some of the deeper reasons, I imagine the ticket sounds simple but it actually quite complex, and I'm not in disagreement of that. 

My usecase is i'm developing a Virtual Private Network that uses /dev/net/tun but it's intended to run a Container, and now I'm just trying to test if it works! No reason I can't use a Virtual Machine created through VirtualBox, HyperV, Just thought it would be nice to be able to do it with Podman :)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
